bio.quote: Kaelen, called The Seventh, is a broken hero bound to Svellheim’s crisis. He carries guilt that won’t thaw: guest-right broken, a rite mispaid, a name spoken at the wrong root-junction.In combat, Kael

system.description:
Humans

Stereotype: Quick to build, quick to die, quick to break their word.

Size: 1M; Speed: 5; Resistance: 0

novice.description:
Humans

Stereotype: Quick to build, quick to die, quick to break their word.

Size: 1M; Speed: 5; Resistance: 0

system.description: As a free action, you can open your awareness to detect supernatural creatures and phenomena. Until the end of your next turn, you know the location of any supernatural object, or any undead, construct, or creature from another world within 5 meters, even if you don't have line of effect. You know if you're detecting an item or a creature, and its nature.

system.description: Ignore temporary difficult terrain created by magic/psionics. Reduce forced movement from such sources by 1.

novice.description: Ignore temporary difficult terrain created by magic/psionics. Reduce forced movement from such sources by 1.

system.description: When you take the Help action, the bonus granted is +2 instead of +1.

novice.description: When you take the Help action, the bonus granted is +2 instead of +1.

system.description: Advantage (Second Chance) on Endurance tests. Your *slowed* speed is 3 instead of 2.

novice.description: Advantage (Second Chance) on Endurance tests. Your *slowed* speed is 3 instead of 2.

system.description:
Nomad lines that winter beside rib-cairns and bone-fields, keeping the old fires and the old taboos while carrying authority by rite and remembrance.

Language: Beinmarksmål

Environment: Nomadic; Organization: Communal; Upbringing: Noble

system.description:
Skills: Cunning/Lore, Vigilant, Cunning.

Languages: One additional language.

Renown: 1; Wealth: 0; Project Points: 150

Perk: *True Omen*. Once per session, after you study signs or consult a rite, gain an advantage (second chance) on one test to interpret a supernatural or symbolic clue.

novice.description:
Skills: Cunning/Lore, Vigilant, Cunning.

Languages: One additional language.

Renown: 1; Wealth: 0; Project Points: 150

Perk: *True Omen*. Once per session, after you study signs or consult a rite, gain an advantage (second chance) on one test to interpret a supernatural or symbolic clue.

system.description: You automatically learn two new languages, as long as you have regularly heard those languages spoken or seen them written before. Additionally, if you spend 7 days or more in a place where you regularly hear or read a language you don’t know, you can pick up enough of that language to hold a conversation or understand basic written information. Having picked up a language this way, you can subsequently learn it using the Learn New Language research project at half the usual project goal cost (see Chapter 12: Downtime Projects).

novice.description: You automatically learn two new languages, as long as you have regularly heard those languages spoken or seen them written before. Additionally, if you spend 7 days or more in a place where you regularly hear or read a language you don’t know, you can pick up enough of that language to hold a conversation or understand basic written information. system.description: The power of the gods flows through you! As a vessel for divine power, you don’t just keep your allies in the fight. You make those allies more effective, even as you rain divine energy down upon your foes. Though the deity or saint you serve might have other faithful and clergy, you are special among worshippers, receiving your abilities from the highest source. As a conduit, you heal and buff your allies, and debuff your foes while smiting them with divine magic. The spark of divinity within you shines, filling your enemies with awe and making you more worldly and aware.

The spark of divinity within you shines, filling your enemies with awe and making you more worldly and aware.

system.description: Your deity is aware of your growing influence, making it easier to draw their attention and power when you heal your allies. Whenever you allow another creature to spend a Recovery Test, you can also spend a Recovery Test.

novice.description: Your deity is aware of your growing influence, making it easier to draw their attention and power when you heal your allies. Whenever you allow another creature to spend a Recovery Test, you can also spend a Recovery Test.

system.description:
A supernatural spirit who has taken the form of a specific small animal or animated object has chosen to be your familiar—or to adopt you as their familiar.

The spirit uses the @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.monsters.Actor.cBkB3TyhBE1UsNGM]{familiar} stat block.

The familiar can hold small objects in their mouth or claws, but can’t perform activities that would typically require hands (opening a door, unrolling a scroll, and so forth). They can’t harm other creatures or objects. They can flank in combat, but only with you.

If your familiar is destroyed, you can restore them as a extended rest activity, or by spending a Recovery Test as a main action to bring them back into existence in an unoccupied space adjacent to you.

system.description: As a extended rest activity, you can bless a weapon. Any creature who wields the weapon gains a +1 bonus to rolled damage with abilities that use the weapon. This benefit lasts until you finish another extended rest.

novice.description: As a extended rest activity, you can bless a weapon. Any creature who wields the weapon gains a +1 bonus to rolled damage with abilities that use the weapon. This benefit lasts until you finish another extended rest.

system.description: Whenever you gain piety from a domain effect, you gain 1 additional piety.

novice.description: Whenever you gain piety from a domain effect, you gain 1 additional piety.

system.description:
Piety: You {gain 2 piety} the first time in an encounter that an enemy within 10 meters takes fire or holy damage.

Prayer Effect: One enemy within 10 meters takes {fire damage} equal to three times your Intuition score.

novice.description:
Piety: You {gain 2 piety} the first time in an encounter that an enemy within 10 meters takes fire or holy damage.

Prayer Effect: One enemy within 10 meters takes {fire damage} equal to three times your Intuition score.

system.description:
Piety: You gain 2 piety the first time in an encounter that a creature within 10 meters uses an area ability.

Prayer Effect: You summon the forces of creation and create a wall of stone within 10 meters whose size is 5 + your Intuition score. The wall lasts until the end of the encounter.

novice.description:
Piety: You gain 2 piety the first time in an encounter that a creature within 10 meters uses an area ability.

Prayer Effect: You summon the forces of creation and create a wall of stone within 10 meters whose size is 5 + your Intuition score. The wall lasts until the end of the encounter.

system.description: At the start of a extended rest, you can inspire yourself or another creature taking the same extended rest with divine knowledge. If the target makes a project roll during this extended rest, they can add 1d10 plus your Intuition score to the roll.

novice.description: At the start of a extended rest, you can inspire yourself or another creature taking the same extended rest with divine knowledge. If the target makes a project roll during this extended rest, they can add 1d10 plus your Intuition score to the roll.

system.description:
Your deity grants you a Heroic Resource called piety, letting you heal and empower your allies, and unleash holy power upon your foes.

Piety in Combat

At the start of a combat encounter or some other stressful situation tracked in combat rounds (as determined by the Director), you gain piety equal to [[lookup @hero.milestones]]{your Milestones}. At the start of each of your turns during combat, you gain 1d3 piety.

Additionally, you can gain more piety by praying to the gods—but beware! Doing so can easily draw their ire, as the gods hate to be annoyed. Before you roll to gain piety at the start of your turn, you can pray (no action required). If you do, your roll gains the following additional effects:

  • If the roll is a 1, you gain 1 additional piety but anger the gods! You take psychic damage equal to {1d6 + your level}, which can’t be reduced in any way.

  • If the roll is a 2, you {gain 1 additional piety}.

  • If the roll is a 3, you {gain 2 additional piety} and can activate a domain effect of your choice (see below).

You lose any remaining piety at the end of the encounter.

Piety Outside of Combat

Though you can’t gain piety outside of combat, you can use your heroic abilities and effects that cost piety without spending it. Whenever you use an ability or effect outside of combat that costs piety, you can’t use that same ability or effect outside of combat again until you earn 1 or more Milestones or finish a extended rest.

When you use an ability outside of combat that lets you spend unlimited piety on its effect, such as Healing Grace, you can use it as if you had spent an amount of piety equal to your Milestones.

Domain Piety and Effects

Your choice of domains provides you with two additional ways to earn piety during combat, as triggered by specific events. You might even have a single event trigger both your piety effects. For example, the Sun domain grants piety when a nearby creature takes fire or holy damage, while the War domain grants piety when a nearby creature takes damage of 10 + your level or higher. If you have both those domains and a nearby creature takes an appropriate amount of fire damage, you gain piety from both your domains.

Additionally, whenever you activate a domain effect by praying for piety, you can choose one of your domains and have that domain’s prayer effect take effect immediately.

Domain Piety and Effects

system.description: You can interpret diagrams and charts even if you don’t understand the language associated with them. You are considered fluent in all languages for the purpose of understanding the project source for any research or crafting project.

novice.description: You can interpret diagrams and charts even if you don’t understand the language associated with them. You are considered fluent in all languages for the purpose of understanding the project source for any research or crafting project.

system.description:
As a extended rest activity, you can perform a religious ritual and beseech the gods to restore a dead creature to life. You must have at least half the creature’s remains, and they must have died within the last 24 hours from an effect that isn’t age related. The creature’s soul must be willing to return to life for the ritual to work. If they are not willing, you instinctively understand that as you start the extended rest activity and can cease it immediately.

A creature with a willing soul returns to life at the end of the extended rest with full Toughness and half their Recovery Tests. You regain only half your Recovery Tests at the end of the extended rest.

novice.description:

You must have at least half the creature’s remains, and they must have died within the last 24 hours from an effect that isn’t age related. The creature’s soul must be willing to return to life for the ritual to work. If they are not willing, you instinctively understand that as you start the extended rest activity and can cease it immediately.

A creature with a willing soul returns to life at the end of the extended rest with full Toughness and half their Recovery Tests. You regain only half your Recovery Tests at the end of the extended rest.

system.description: Whenever you fail a test to recall lore using a skill from the lore skill group, you instinctively recall the nearest location where the information you seek might be found. This could be the tower of a local sage, a library in a nearby city, somewhere deep in a dungeon, or any other location of the Director’s determination. The Director can decide that certain lore can’t be revealed this way.

novice.description: Whenever you fail a test to recall lore using a skill from the lore skill group, you instinctively recall the nearest location where the information you seek might be found. This could be the tower of a local sage, a library in a nearby city, somewhere deep in a dungeon, or any other location of the Director’s determination. The Director can decide that certain lore can’t be revealed this way.

system.description: Your god fills your body with the light of creation, making you harder to hurt and move. You gain a +6 bonus to Toughness, and this bonus increases by 6 at 4th, 7th, and 10th levels. Additionally, you gain a +1 bonus to resistance.

novice.description: Your god fills your body with the light of creation, making you harder to hurt and move. You gain a +6 bonus to Toughness, and this bonus increases by 6 at 4th, 7th, and 10th levels. Additionally, you gain a +1 bonus to resistance.

system.description: The gods imbue a divine swiftness within you. Whenever an adjacent creature deals damage to you, you can shift up to a number of meters equal to [[lookup @I]]{your Intuition score} after the damage is dealt.

novice.description: The gods imbue a divine swiftness within you. Whenever an adjacent creature deals damage to you, you can shift up to a number of meters equal to [[lookup @I]]{your Intuition score} after the damage is dealt.

system.description: When you use your @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.classes.Item.QDpxxvypQpiRqR3t]{Hands of the Maker} ability, you can create a mundane object that is size 2 or smaller.

novice.description: When you use your @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.classes.Item.QDpxxvypQpiRqR3t]{Hands of the Maker} ability, you can create a mundane object that is size 2 or smaller.
